global.title: A Powerhouse project has four main directories: document-models, editors, processors, and subgraphs
global.content:
A Powerhouse project initialized with `ph init` creates this structure:

- `document-models/` - Document model implementations (schema, gen/, src/reducers, src/tests)
- `editors/` - React editor components for document types
- `processors/` - Event-driven components reacting to document changes
- `subgraphs/` - Modular GraphQL services for structured data access

Configuration files:
- `powerhouse.config.json` - Project and Vetra drive configuration
- `powerhouse.manifest.json` - Package manifest describing contents
- `package.json` - NPM package metadata (scoped naming: `@org-ph/package-name`)
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Build configuration
- `vitest.config.ts` - Test configuration

For publishing, the manifest.json declares document models, editors, and their relationships (which editor serves which document type).
global.description: ph init scaffolds document-models/, editors/, processors/, subgraphs/ plus config files for a complete package structure.
